title: Ideal lead signal
body: Magnio is most credible when the lead has an expensive workflow problem, clear friction, and a business owner who wants speed with operational discipline. Good lead signals include manual work, disconnected systems, back-office delays, poor visibility, or AI interest without a practical execution plan.

title: Strong buying signals
body: Strong Magnio buying signals include a workflow with measurable cost or delay, executive sponsorship, a willingness to pilot before scale, and a need to connect systems rather than buy another generic dashboard. These leads usually care about throughput, visibility, and decision quality.

title: Weak fit signals
body: Weak fit signals include purely experimental curiosity with no owner, a request for AI branding without workflow substance, or a desire to automate a process that has not been defined. If the ask is vague and there is no measurable outcome, Magnio should narrow the scope before proposing a build.

title: Best inputs for a first call
body: The most useful first-call inputs are the workflow that hurts, the systems involved, who owns the decision, the current delay or cost, and what a successful pilot would change. Magnio should use those inputs to decide whether the right next step is discovery, a pilot, or a lighter automation project.
